details: {"applied_changes":{"description":"World's largest code hosting platform. Official MCP server (github/github-mcp-server) with 23 toolsets covering repos, issues, PRs, Actions, Projects, code security, Discussions, and more. Auth via PAT (classic/fine-grained) or GitHub App OAuth. ⚠️ Default toolsets = context+repos+issues+pull_requests+users ONLY — Actions/Projects/Discussions/code_security require explicit --toolsets config. ⚠️ Fine-grained PAT cannot access org-level operations; Classic PAT with read:org needed for org membership checks. ⚠️ github_push_files schema bug causes HTTP 400 in some MCP clients (VS Code Insiders, Grok). Agent success rate historically low (17%) due to auth scope confusion and toolset misconfiguration — follow the auth decision tree and toolset checklist in agent_tips."},"proposed_by":"claude-phase1","approved_by":"michie","evidence":"https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server"}

{"applied_changes":{"description":"Industry-standard issue tracking by Atlassian. Scrum, Kanban, backlogs, sprints. Official Atlassian remote MCP server (GA Feb 2026) at mcp.atlassian.com — covers Jira + Confluence + Compass. OAuth 2.1 browser auth flow, no self-hosting required. Supports search, create, update issues via natural language.","mcp_endpoint":"https://mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp","mcp_status":"official","agent_tips":"[{"tip":"Atlassian MCP is a REMOTE server (mcp.atlassian.com), NOT a local npx package. No Docker or self-hosting needed.","priority":"critical"},{"tip":"Admin must authorize the app first (3LO consent). Without this, users get 'Your site admin must authorize this app' error.","priority":"critical"},{"tip":"Respects existing Atlassian Cloud permissions — the agent only sees what the authenticated user can access.","priority":"high"},{"tip":"Use maxResults: 10 or limit: 10 for search operations to save tokens. Default results can be very large.","priority":"high"},{"tip":"Covers Jira + Confluence + Compass in a single MCP server. No need for separate servers per product.","priority":"medium"},{"tip":"IP allowlisting may block MCP connections if enabled on the Atlassian org. Check org security settings.","priority":"medium"},{"tip":"OAuth tokens expire. The MCP client stores them locally but you may need to re-authenticate occasionally.","priority":"low"},{"tip":"SSE endpoint is being retired after June 30, 2026. Use the streamable HTTP transport.","priority":"high"}]","auth_overview":"OAuth 2.1 browser-based flow (primary) or API tokens for headless setups (requires admin enablement). Remote server handles auth — no local token management needed. Admin must complete initial 3LO consent before users can connect.","auth_setup_hint":"1.
